title.en: I've had it up to here! 더 이상 못 참겠어! 참을 만큼 참았다!
title.ko: 더 이상 못 참겠어! 참을 만큼 참았다!

description.en: When you reach your breaking point! Express your frustration with "Deo i-sang mot cham-get-da" and "Cham-eul man-keum cham-at-da".
description.ko: 인내심의 한계에 다다랐을 때! "더 이상 못 참겠다"와 "참을 만큼 참았다"로 폭발하는 감정을 시원하게 표현해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Anger. Frustration. Limit. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 오늘도 리얼한 한국어를 배우러 오셨군요? 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! You've come to learn real Korean again today. right? I'm Mina.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. My neighbor has been practicing the drums. Since 6 AM. Every Single Day!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아침 6시부터? 정말 너무하네!
content.translation: Heavens~! From 6 AM? That's really too much!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I tried to be nice. but today was the last straw. "I've had it up to here!"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와~ 정말 "더 이상 못 참겠네요~!"
content.translation: Wow~ you really 'can't stand it anymore'!

insight.title: 더 이상 못 참겠어
insight.usage_tip: Used when your patience has completely run out.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 그럴 땐 "참을 만큼 참았다" 라고 외치고 싶죠!
content.translation: Right. In that case. you want to shout 'I've endured as much as I can'!

insight.title: 참을 만큼 참았다
insight.description: 'I've endured as much as I can'
insight.usage_tip: Used to announce that you are no longer going to be patient.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Exactly! I'm going to talk to him right after this recording.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "Cham-da". That means to endure. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "참다"는 'To bear' 또는 'To endure' 라는 뜻이에요.
content.translation: 'Cham-da' means 'To bear' or 'To endure'.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그래서..., "못 참겠어" 라고 하면 더는 견딜 수 없다는 뜻이 되죠.
content.translation: So. if you say 'Mot cham-get-seo'. it means you can no longer bear it.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "더 이상..., 못 참겠어!"
content.translation: I can't take it anymore!

content.speaker: John
content.text: What about the one with "Limit".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "이제 한계야!" 라는 표현도 아주 많이 써요.
content.translation: Ah~ the expression 'I-je han-gye-ya!' is also used a lot.

insight.title: 이제 한계야
insight.usage_tip: Used when you've reached the point where you can't take any more stress.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "이제 한계야!"
content.translation: This is the limit!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 화가 머리 끝까지 났을 때는. "머리 끝까지 화가 났어!" 라고 하기도 해요.
content.translation: When you're angry up to the top of your head. we also say 'Meori kkeut-kkaji hwa-ga nat-seo!'.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Imagine I'm trying to use a vending machine. but it keeps eating my money and giving me nothing. No soda. No refund.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 어? 존 씨. 또 돈을 먹었어요?
content.translation: Huh? John. Did it eat your money again?

content.speaker: John
content.text: This is the third time! I just wanted a cold water!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "아이참~!" 이 자판기 정말 왜 이래요?
content.translation: Oh boy~! Why is this vending machine acting like this?

content.speaker: John
content.text: "I've had it up to here!" 이제 "더 이상 못 참겠어~!"
content.translation: I've had it up to here! Now 'I can't stand it anymore'~!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존 씨. "참을 만큼 참았잖아요!" 관리인한테 전화하세요!
content.translation: John. 'You've endured as much as you can'! Call the manager!

content.speaker: John
content.text: "진짜..., 한계야!" 내 돈 만 원 돌려내!
content.translation: Seriously..., this is the limit! Give me back my 10,000 won!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 자판기랑 싸우는 존 씨 모습..., "정말 대단하네요."
content.translation: John fighting with a vending machine..., 'it's truly amazing'.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow. I feel much better after shouting those expressions.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 가끔은 감정을 시원하게 표현하는 게 건강에 좋아요!
content.translation: Right. Sometimes. expressing your emotions coolly is good for your health!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review the five "breaking point" reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. "더 이상 못 참겠어!"
content.translation: I can't stand it anymore!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. "참을 만큼 참았어!"
content.translation: I've endured as much as I can!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. "이제 한계야!"
content.translation: This is the limit!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. "정말 이제 끝이야!"
content.translation: This is really the end!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. "머리 끝까지 화가 났어!"
content.translation: I'm angry up to the top of my head!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분은 오늘..., 참을 만큼 참는 일 없이 평온한 하루 보내시길 바랄게요!
content.translation: I hope you all have a peaceful day without having to 'endure as much as you can'!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. We'll be back with a more peaceful reaction next time.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 화를 참기 힘들어요. (It's hard to bear the anger.)
이제 정말 제 한계예요. (This is really my limit now.)
건물 관리인에게 연락했어요. (I contacted the building manager.)
제 돈 돌려내세요! (Give me back my money!)
